generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time environmental sensor for monitoring temperature and humidity levels in LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, integrated with IoT networks for dynamic indoor environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureHumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance AirQualitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision sensor for monitoring indoor air quality parameters (e.g., CO2, particulate matter) in University Library, supporting real-time monitoring of sensitive materials."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motion and presence detection sensor for tracking occupancy patterns in academic/research facilities, enabling dynamic control of lighting and HVAC systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralIntegritySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring sensor for detecting vibrations, stress, and integrity changes in building infrastructure, integrated with University Library's fire safety systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyManagementSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy consumption and efficiency monitoring sensor for HVAC and lighting systems in LEED Platinum Academic Building, supporting real-time energy optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyManagementDataTransmitted
]

instance IoTSensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Distributed IoT sensor network integrating all environmental and structural monitoring sensors across LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library, enabling real-time data collection for digital twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData IoTSensorNetworkDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureHumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureHumiditySensor
]

instance AirQualityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirQualitySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralIntegritySensor
]

instance EnergyManagementDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyManagementSensor
]

instance IoTSensorNetworkDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IoTSensorNetwork
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically rich, object-based 3D model encapsulating geometric, spatial, and functional characteristics of building components (LEED Platinum Academic Building and University Library) as per Building Information Modeling (BIM) standards."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance IndoorEnvironmentalSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation model for indoor conditions (thermal dynamics, airflow) enabling what-if analysis and occupant behavior simulations in academic/research facilities."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineEnabler, PythonJuliaScriptingEnabler
]

instance EnergyOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Adaptive control algorithm model for dynamically adjusting lighting, HVAC, and shading systems based on real-time data and predefined objectives (e.g., energy savings, comfort levels)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MachineLearningEnabler, RuleEngineEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously synchronized operational data from IoT sensors (temperature, humidity, occupancy, air quality, structural integrity) feeding into the digital twin framework for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureHumidityDataTransmitted, AirQualityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted, EnergyManagementDataTransmitted, IoTSensorNetworkDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series historical data of building operations (energy consumption, maintenance records, environmental conditions) used for trend analysis and predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler
]

instance GeospatialData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Geospatial data including CAD/BIM models and GIS information for large-scale infrastructure projects, enabling integration with civil engineering and urban planning systems."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight MQTT broker for secure, scalable IoT data ingestion from sensors, facilitating real-time synchronization between physical assets and digital twins."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DataIngestionService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin registry managing asset metadata, state synchronization, and event-driven updates between physical and virtual models, ensuring bidirectional data exchange."
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalTwinSynchronizationService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D visualization engine providing immersive monitoring, physics simulations, and interactive scenario testing for indoor environments in academic/research facilities."
    DTDFVocab:enables ImmersiveVisualizationService, ScenarioTestingService
]

instance PythonJuliaScriptingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom scripting environment for physics-based modeling (thermal, airflow) and optimization algorithms, enabling dynamic system behavior predictions."
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicsSimulationService
]

instance MachineLearningEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning framework for integrating predictive maintenance and energy optimization algorithms into the digital twin, providing data-driven recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService, EnergyOptimizationService
]

instance RuleEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated rule engine for dynamically adjusting building systems (lighting, HVAC) based on real-time data and predefined control logic."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveControlService
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database for structured storage and retrieval of sensor data, supporting historical trend analysis and predictive modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalTrendAnalysisService
]

instance KubernetesOrchestrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Container orchestration platform for deploying and managing digital twin services, ensuring scalability and high availability of real-time applications."
    DTDFVocab:enables ServiceDeploymentService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking of indoor environmental parameters (temperature, humidity, CO2, occupancy) via sensor networks and data ingestion pipelines, providing dashboard-based visualization and alerting mechanisms."
    DTDFVocab:provides IndoorEnvironmentInsight, ThresholdBreachAlertAction
]

instance PhysicsSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Physics-based modeling of indoor conditions (thermal dynamics, airflow) to predict system behavior under varying scenarios, enabling what-if analysis for energy-efficient strategies."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalDynamicInsight, AirflowPatternInsight
]

instance AdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of lighting, HVAC, and shading systems based on real-time data and predefined objectives (e.g., energy savings, comfort levels) using automated rule engines."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight, OccupantComfortInsight, AdjustLightingAction, AdjustHVACAction
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D environments for real-time monitoring, scenario testing, and user interaction, enabling multi-user collaboration for researchers and facility managers."
    DTDFVocab:provides Interactive3DInsight, CollaborativeScenarioInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics for identifying potential equipment failures and maintenance needs based on real-time and historical operational data."
    DTDFVocab:provides EquipmentFailureInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance EnergyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data-driven optimization of energy consumption through machine learning and adaptive control algorithms, providing recommendations for energy-efficient operations."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyConsumptionInsight, OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance DigitalTwinSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Bidirectional synchronization mechanism ensuring continuous data exchange between physical assets and their digital twins, maintaining up-to-date virtual replicas."
    DTDFVocab:provides SynchronizationStatusInsight
]

instance DataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Secure ingestion of real-time sensor data into the digital twin framework, enabling live synchronization with physical infrastructure."

]

instance HistoricalTrendAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

]

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system enables **control flows from the virtual to physical domain** through a **bidirectional communication framework** managed by the DT Framework. Facility Managers interact with the deployed DT to issue **real-time control commands**, such as adjusting HVAC systems, lighting, or security protocols, which are translated into executable actions for physical devices. While the document does not specify **command types** (e.g., setpoints, binary on/off signals) or **message formats** (e.g., JSON/XML), the system supports **validated control logic** to ensure commands align with operational constraints. **Feedback systems** are implied through the bidirectional exchange, where physical device responses (e.g., status updates, error signals) are relayed back to the DT for continuous monitoring. **Conflict resolution** and **command execution confirmation** are not explicitly described, but the framework’s **runtime configuration management** suggests dynamic adjustments to control logic without redeployment, potentially mitigating conflicts. Authentication and validation mechanisms are not detailed, though the system’s integration with BIM tools implies a structured approach to device modeling and deployment, which may include implicit validation."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts. This synchronization is foundational for operational monitoring and control, enabling dynamic adjustments without requiring full redeployment. The framework supports **runtime configuration management**, allowing for adaptive adjustments to operational requirements. While specific synchronization frequencies (e.g., sub-second or millisecond-level latency) are not detailed, the system is designed to bridge the gap between static BIM representations and dynamic operational needs, implying a focus on **low-latency, high-fidelity synchronization** for critical building systems. The temporal granularity is implicitly tied to the operational context, ensuring alignment with real-time decision-making processes in the AECO domain (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is structured around a **service-oriented architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling the integration of custom services and analytics. This modular design allows the Digital Twin to evolve dynamically, accommodating changing operational requirements. The system is orchestrated through a **central DT Framework** that facilitates bidirectional data exchange and holistic building life cycle management. Key components include enriched BIM models (as input) and operational Digital Twins (as output), with roles assigned to actors (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) to ensure coordinated contributions across the Digital Twin lifecycle. The architecture emphasizes **interoperability** and **adaptability**, though specific behaviors like hot-swapped services or seasonal energy optimization are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **runtime operation**, addressing the gap between static BIM representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:

1. **Device Modeling & BIM Integration**:
   - Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** directly within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with **semantic representations** of Building Control System (BCS) components.
   - This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations, leveraging BIM’s geometric and spatial data while addressing its limitations in dynamic system modeling.

2. **Model Transformation & Artifact Generation**:
   - The framework employs **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles** to extract **structured representations** from enriched BIM models.
   - Automated generation of **deployable DT artifacts** (e.g., simulation models, control logic templates) occurs with **minimal manual intervention**, ensuring consistency between physical and digital representations.

3. **DT Deployment & Synchronization**:
   - The framework supports **real-time data exchange** between physical buildings and their digital twins, enabling continuous synchronization of system states.
   - **Interactive visualization interfaces** provide immersive monitoring and control capabilities, tailored for Facility Managers and Building Control Engineers.

4. **Runtime Operation & Evolution**:
   - The DT operates in a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, allowing integration of **custom services, analytics, and plugins** developed by DT Engineers.
   - **Lifecycle governance** is implicit through the framework’s support for **dynamic adaptation** to changing operational requirements, ensuring the DT evolves alongside the physical system.

Transition management between phases is facilitated by:
- **Automated workflows** for model-to-artifact conversion (MDE-driven).
- **Role-specific access** (e.g., Building Control Engineers for modeling, Facility Managers for monitoring).
- **Version control** implied by the framework’s modularity and extension points, though explicit versioning mechanisms are not detailed in the document.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity and validity** through **semantic integration** and **real-time synchronization**, ensuring alignment between **design-time BIM models** and **runtime operational data**. Validation is implicitly supported by the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which evaluates the **entanglement** between physical and digital representations to quantify fidelity. The system’s **model-driven automation (R2)** and **modular architecture (R4)** enable **reproducible DT setups**, reducing manual intervention and improving consistency. While the document does not explicitly detail **uncertainty sources** (e.g., sensor noise, model approximation), it highlights the **gap between design-time and runtime** as a critical consideration. **Quality assurance** is embedded in the **DSR-driven framework**, with **real-time data integration (R3)** mitigating latency and ensuring data accuracy. No explicit **error detection/correction mechanisms** or **quantitative uncertainty quantification** methods are described."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT system employs a **modular, service-oriented architecture** with a **central hub (DT Framework)** to facilitate interoperability across heterogeneous systems. The framework supports **standardized extension points** for integrating custom services, enabling seamless communication between physical and virtual components. While the document does not specify **communication protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or **network topology** (e.g., star, mesh), the system relies on **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for data exchange, suggesting a **lightweight, event-driven** approach to handle real-time synchronization. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are not quantified, but the emphasis on **real-time data exchange** implies low-latency constraints for critical applications. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not explicitly described, though the framework’s **scalability** and **maintainability** requirements (NF_X) suggest fault-tolerant design principles. **Security protocols** for network communication are not mentioned, but the system’s integration with BIM tools and operational data implies adherence to industry-standard security practices for building automation systems."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system is deployed using a **model-driven framework** that supports **scalable Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle management**, bridging static BIM representations with dynamic operational needs. The framework enables **modular deployment** of DTs, allowing for **runtime configuration management** to adapt to evolving requirements without full redeployment. While the document does not specify **hosting platforms** (e.g., cloud, edge, on-premise) or **computational/storage requirements**, the system’s scalability is emphasized, suggesting support for **distributed deployment models** to handle infrastructure-wide operations. **Deployment models** are not detailed, but the framework’s **standardized extension points** imply compatibility with various deployment strategies, including hybrid or edge-based configurations. **Availability and reliability assurance** are addressed through the framework’s **low-overhead updates** and **maintainability** requirements (NF_X), ensuring minimal downtime and seamless integration with existing AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) workflows."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, ensuring seamless integration with external tools and platforms. The framework leverages **BIM (Building Information Modeling)** as a foundational data source, transforming static BIM models into dynamic operational Digital Twins. While specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, Modbus) or file formats (e.g., IFC) are not mentioned, the system is designed to support **data exchange protocols** and **interoperability standards** that enable cross-system compatibility. Dependencies on external systems are implied through the need for bidirectional synchronization with physical building components, though no explicit enterprise systems (e.g., ERP, CMMS) or third-party Digital Twins are referenced. The integration pattern is centered on the DT Framework acting as a **central hub** for unified data management."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **industry standards and interoperability protocols** to ensure **seamless integration**, **compliance**, and **scalability**, with a focus on **BIM as the foundational standard** and **modular, extensible architectures**:
  - **BIM as Standard**: Leverages **Building Information Modeling (BIM)** as the **primary standard** for device modeling and DT deployment, addressing its limitations through **semantic enrichment** and **MDE-driven transformations**. BIM’s **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes)** or **COBie (Construction Operations Building Information Exchange)** formats may be used for **data exchange**.
  - **Interoperability Standards**:
    - **Connectivity**: Employs **Eclipse Ditto** (or similar) for **robust, standardized connectivity** between physical devices and the DT, ensuring **protocol-agnostic integration** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT).
    - **Modular Architecture**: Defines **standardized extension points** for **plug-and-play integration** of custom services, adhering to **open APIs** and **service contracts** (e.g., RESTful interfaces, event-driven architectures).
  - **Compliance Frameworks**:
    - **Functional Requirements (F_X)**: Aligns with **research-derived requirements** (e.g., from TwinLight, BOREALIS projects) to ensure **operational consistency** and **stakeholder alignment**.
    - **Lifecycle Governance**: Supports **certification-ready processes** via **role-based access controls**, **audit trails**, and **versioned artifacts**.
  - **Certification & Validation**:
    - **Automated Compliance Checks**: MDE transformations include **semantic validation** to ensure artifacts meet **standardized schemas** (e.g., BIM extensions for DTs).
    - **Runtime Monitoring**: Validates **real-time synchronization compliance** with **physical-digital alignment** requirements.
  - **Industry Specifications**:
    - **Building Automation**: Aligns with **ASHRAE, ISO 16484 (BMS), or CEN standards** for building control systems.
    - **Digital Twin Standards**: May incorporate **ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42** (AI/ML) or **NIST Digital Twin Framework** for **cross-domain interoperability**.
  - **Notable Tools/Technologies**:
    - **Eclipse Ditto**: Provides **standard-compliant connectivity** and **device management**.
    - **MDE Tools**: Use **model transformation languages** (e.g., ATL, QVT) to ensure **standardized artifact generation**.
  - **Interoperability**: Ensures **cross-platform compatibility** via **open standards** (e.g., OPC UA for industrial IoT, Web Services for cloud integration)."
]
